docs(security): Comprehensive Security Audit - March 2026 (#51)

* fix: real-time competitor sync — cache live events and add retry

The live subscription was discarding received Nostr events and
re-fetching from relays via syncCompetitor(). Due to relay propagation
delays (event arrives from relay X, query hits relay Y), the re-fetch
often returned stale data, causing silent sync failures with no retry.

Changes:
- Cache full ActionLogEntry objects from live subscription instead of
  just pubkey strings, and pass them to syncCompetitor()
- Merge cached live events into the fetched action log (deduped by
  event ID) so events not yet indexed by relays are still replayed
- Add retry logic to syncCompetitor() (up to 2 attempts with 3s delay)
  when live events were received but relay fetch returned empty
- Cache global actions from syncWorld() for 60s to avoid redundant
  10K-event fetches in syncCompetitor()
- Add diagnostic logging to all silent failure paths (empty actions,
  null airline after replay, sync failures)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(store): address PR #47 review feedback

- replay buffered live events for known competitors
- refresh global actions cache on miss
- add syncCompetitor regression tests for liveEvents/retry

* fix(store): dedupe realtime sync events

RedisCache (cache.py):

- Atomic pipeline for put; LRU eviction when over max_size

fix: enforce blocking hook exit status for Pi Agent (#22)

* fix: line count tracking for file overwrites and blocking hook support

When Pi Agent uses the 'write' tool to overwrite a file, it only sends
the new content without the old content. This caused gryph to treat every
write as a new file creation, missing the lines_removed count.

Changes:
- Read existing file content to calculate proper lines_added/lines_removed
- Use oldText/newText fields (Pi Agent format) for edit tool parsing
- Add buildPayloadForResult to avoid duplicating write details in tool_result
- Skip tool_result for successful file operations to prevent duplicate events
- Add synchronous hook execution with spawnSync for blocking decisions
- Add 30s timeout to prevent agent freeze if hook hangs
- Add test for edit tool with oldText/newText fields

* feat(workspace): Add pnpm workspace configuration with ignored built dependencies

* Add best practices for React and server-side development

- Introduced guidelines for calculating derived state during rendering to avoid redundant renders.
- Added recommendations for subscribing to derived state to reduce re-render frequency.
- Emphasized the use of functional setState updates to prevent stale closures and unnecessary callback recreations.
- Suggested lazy state initialization for expensive initial values to optimize performance.
- Provided guidance on extracting default non-primitive parameter values from memoized components to restore memoization.
- Encouraged extracting expensive work into memoized components to enable early returns.
- Recommended placing interaction logic in event handlers to avoid unnecessary effect re-runs.
- Advised against wrapping simple expressions in useMemo to prevent wasted computation.
- Promoted the use of transitions for non-urgent updates to maintain UI responsiveness.
- Suggested using useRef for transient values to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
- Introduced the use of after() for non-blocking operations in server actions.
- Highlighted the importance of authenticating server actions like API routes to prevent unauthorized access.
- Implemented cross-request LRU caching for shared data across sequential requests.
- Recommended per-request deduplication with React.cache() for efficient data fetching.
- Advised avoiding duplicate serialization in RSC props to reduce network payload.
- Encouraged parallel data fetching with component composition to eliminate server-side waterfalls.
- Suggested minimizing serialization at RSC boundaries to reduce data transfer size.
- Added a new skill for reviewing UI code against Web Interface Guidelines compliance.

- Recommended per-request deduplication with React.cache() for efficient data fetching.

- Introduced guidelines for calculating derived state during rendering to avoid redundant renders.
- Added recommendations for subscribing to derived state to reduce re-render frequency.
- Emphasized the use of functional setState updates to prevent stale closures and unnecessary callback recreations.
- Suggested lazy state initialization for expensive initial values to optimize performance.
- Provided guidance on extracting default non-primitive parameter values from memoized components to restore memoization.
- Encouraged extracting expensive work into memoized components to enable early returns.
- Recommended placing interaction logic in event handlers to avoid unnecessary effect re-runs.
- Advised against wrapping simple expressions in useMemo to prevent wasted computation.
- Promoted the use of transitions for non-urgent updates to maintain UI responsiveness.
- Suggested using useRef for transient values to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
- Introduced the use of after() for non-blocking operations in server actions.
- Highlighted the importance of authenticating server actions like API routes to prevent unauthorized access.
- Implemented cross-request LRU caching for shared data across sequential requests.
- Recommended per-request deduplication with React.cache() for efficient data fetching.
- Advised avoiding duplicate serialization in RSC props to reduce network payload.
- Encouraged parallel data fetching with component composition to eliminate server-side waterfalls.
- Suggested minimizing serialization at RSC boundaries to reduce data transfer size.

commit.message:
fix(apple-silicon): suppress Rust 2024 unsafe/lint warnings in 23 NEON files (#3265)

## Summary

Eliminates **365 compile errors** when building bitnet-kernels with
`RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings"` on aarch64/Apple Silicon.

### Problem

Rust 2024 edition enforces `unsafe_op_in_unsafe_fn` by default,
requiring explicit `unsafe {}` blocks inside `unsafe fn` bodies. All 23
NEON kernel files had a mix of:
- Missing `unsafe {}` around truly unsafe operations (vld1q_f32,
vst1q_f32, ptr::add, raw pointer deref)
- Unnecessary `unsafe {}` around safe NEON intrinsics (vdupq_n_f32,
vaddq_f32, etc.)
- Dead code, unused variables, unused assignments

### Fix


### Verification

```bash
# Before: 365 errors
RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo check --workspace --no-default-features --features cpu

# After: 0 errors ✅
RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo check --workspace --no-default-features --features cpu
RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo check --workspace --no-default-features --features cpu,full-cli
```

### Files (23)

All in `crates/bitnet-kernels/src/cpu/`:
neon_attention_mask, neon_batch_matmul, neon_conv1d,
neon_dynamic_quantization, neon_embedding_ops, neon_flash_attn_v2,
neon_fma_ops, neon_gelu_ops, neon_group_query_attention,
neon_matmul_tiling, neon_pipeline_scheduler, neon_pooling,
neon_quantized_activation, neon_quantized_ffn, neon_quantized_matmul_v2,
neon_quantized_softmax, neon_residual_ops, neon_sliding_window_attn,
neon_sparse_matmul, neon_token_embedding, neon_vectorized_search,
neon_weight_dequant, simd_attention_mask

### Impact

- **Unblocks Apple Silicon development**: devs can now build with `-D
warnings` on macOS/arm64
- **No behavior change**: only lint suppression, zero functional changes
- **x86_64 neutral**: NEON files are behind `#[cfg(target_arch =
"aarch64")]`, no effect on CI

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Chores**
* Updated internal code quality and compiler configuration across
multiple CPU kernel modules to enhance development stability and
maintainability.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

feat: multiproject isolation, heartbeat optimization, and crew status improvements (#74)

## Summary

Comprehensive command system overhaul, UI improvements, and developer
experience enhancements.

## Command System

### AI-Agent Friendly Naming
- All bare `id` fields renamed to typed IDs: `taskId`, `agentId`,
`timerId`, `issueId`
- All field names standardized to camelCase: `depends_on` → `dependsOn`,
`sourceFile` → `filePath`
- CANCEL_TIMER `name` → `label` (matches SET_TIMER)
- Internal command names aligned: `AGENT_MSG` → `AGENT_MESSAGE`,
`GROUP_MSG` → `GROUP_MESSAGE`, `LOCK` → `LOCK_FILE`, `UNLOCK` →
`UNLOCK_FILE`
- Legacy aliases removed (no backwards compat — clean break)
- Design principle documented in `packages/docs/guide/command-design.md`

### Zod→CommandArg Derivation (Single Source of Truth)
- `.describe()` added to all Zod schema fields (~60 fields across 35
schemas)
- `deriveArgs()` utility introspects Zod schemas to auto-generate help
metadata
- Replaced all hand-maintained `args` arrays in 11 command modules
- Handles: string, number, boolean, array, enum, union, pipe/transform,
optional, ZodDefault
- Override mechanism for edge cases
- 32+ derivation tests including drift detection for all 35 schemas and
Zod internal pin tests
- Net: ~50 lines added (utility), ~150 lines removed (manual metadata)

### Structured Command Help
- `CommandArg` interface: name, type, required, description, default
- Args render as `<required: type>` / `[optional: type = default]`
- All 37+ commands with JSON payloads have structured args
- Template commands included in help output

### Centralized Validation
- All 38 payload handlers use `parseCommandPayload()` (Zod validation
before handler)
- Only 1 justified manual check (COMPLETE_TASK role-conditional
requirement)

## Reviewer System

### Google Eng-Practices Integration
- Code-reviewer: every-line policy, test quality, WHY not WHAT comments,
encouragement
- Critical-reviewer: over-engineering flags, code health, architecture
focus
- Both based on Google eng-practices reviewer guide

### Readability-Reviewer (New Role)
- 14th built-in role in RoleRegistry
- Focus: naming, organization, docs, simplicity, consistency
- Doc freshness checking (project-agnostic)
- All 3 reviewers cross-reference each other
- Lead prompt updated to require triple review

### Test Verification
- Bidirectional in code-reviewer: flag untested new changes + verify
existing tests updated
- "Missing tests in either direction are a review failure"

## UI Improvements

### Interrupt Flow
- Interrupt button works with empty text (calls `/interrupt` endpoint
directly)
- Ctrl+Enter guarded against empty text (prevents accidental interrupts)
- Keyboard shortcut with text → `/message` with `mode: 'interrupt'`
- Added interrupt button to agent chat panel
- LeadDashboard agent popup uses consistent `apiFetch` (was raw `fetch`)

### Dashboard Fixes
- Team panel: model name always right-aligned
- Task DAG: short agent IDs (8 chars) for consistency
- Gantt chart: tooltip clamped to viewport (no overflow)
- Swim lane: layout stability — time range anchored, doesn't collapse on
data updates
- Timeline: legends visible (shrink-0)
- Markdown: code fence rendering in chat messages

### CREW_UPDATE
- Removed false-positive 'stuck agents' warning from ContextRefresher
and SynthesisEngine
- Kept useful context usage warning (>80%)

## Agent Communication
- `@user` instruction added to message formatting for all agents (was
lead-only)
- Agents now prefix user-directed responses with `@user`

## Documentation
- `packages/docs/guide/command-design.md` — AI-agent friendliness
principle, naming conventions, typed IDs, Zod derivation pattern,
new-command checklist
- Fixed stale field names across 5 doc files (30 replacements)
- Updated sidebar navigation

## Multiproject Isolation (#69)
- Agent commands scoped to project
- API routes with `?projectId=` parameter
- WebSocket subscribe-project
- File locks intentionally global (cross-project)
- Sub-agents inherit project from parent lead

## Tests
- 2122+ tests passing across 96 files
- 32 new Zod derivation tests (type extraction, drift detection, Zod pin
tests)
- 10 new markdown rendering tests
- RoleRegistry tests updated (13→14 roles)
- Validation tests updated for all schema changes

---------

fix: normalize MSYS/Git-bash paths on Windows for cwd override

On Windows CI, bash's $(pwd) returns MSYS-style paths like /d/a/repo
instead of D:/a/repo. These paths are invalid for Go's os/filepath
functions, causing discover.Discover() to fail finding workflow files
and returning allow instead of deny.

Add normalizeMSYSPath() to convert /X/path to X:/path on Windows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
